concerns.text: Task T033 (LMM analysis) and T034 (Pearson correlation) are listed as parallel [P] tasks, but T034 depends on the feature importance extraction (T032) and the model training (T030/T031). Furthermore, the Plan explicitly replaces Pearson correlation with LMM for N=2, creating a semantic dependency conflict where T034 implements a method (Pearson) that the Plan (Summary) and T033 (LMM) contradict. T034 cannot be parallel-safe if it relies on the output of T033 or if the statistical method itself is undefined by the current plan.

Task T030 (GNN training) is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T030 depends on the output of T025 (ThermalSample objects with conductivity labels) from Phase 4. While T030 is parallel to other US3 tasks, the [P] tag implies it has no dependencies on other US3 tasks. This is acceptable, but the task description 'predict Static Scattering Potential' contradicts the Spec (FR-004) 'predict local heat flux'. If the implementation follows the Plan (Static Scattering Potential), the data flow from T025 (which contains heat flux/conductivity labels) to T030 is semantically broken, as the model target no longer matches the label source.

Task T022 (Green-Kubo simulation) is marked [P]. However, T022 requires the 'AtomicGraph' or raw XYZ data from T012/T013 (Phase 3). While T022 is parallel to T021 (metrics), the [P] tag on T022 is misleading if it implies independence from Phase 3. More critically, T025 (saving ThermalSample) depends on T022. The ordering within Phase 4 is correct (T021/T022 before T025), but the [P] tag on T022 must be strictly interpreted as 'parallel to T021', not 'parallel to Phase 3'.

Task T035 (statistical power warning) is listed after T034 (correlation analysis). Logically, the power check (N < 10) should occur BEFORE running the correlation analysis (T034) to prevent executing a statistically invalid test. Reordering T035 to precede T034 would improve the logical flow of the data pipeline.
